waysItGetsFooled: NLI 'independent verifier' is NOT independent of the extractor's shortcut (VERIFIED). S0/S8's hardest guarantee is the frozen cross-family DeBERTa NLI checking that a cited span entails the asserted attribute. But NLI models predict entailment largely from lexical overlap between premise and hypothesis (McCoy/HANS 2019; Gururangan hypothesis-only artifacts). The LLM extractor produces an assertion whose surface form is near-copied FROM the span it read -> high lexical overlap -> the NLI checker rubber-stamps it as 'entailed' for exactly the inputs the extractor most easily fabricates by copying surface form. The checker's error is CORRELATED with the extractor's error. 'Different corpus = independent prior' is false: the shared defect is the lexical-overlap shortcut baked into the NLI task, not the training corpus. This is the design's OWN central principle ('an independent critic that shares the blind spot is correlated error relabeled') applied to its own primary non-LLM grounding adversary -- and it is undisclosed.

Cheap grounding is substring-existence, NOT entailment -- it passes polarity/scope inversion. S0 (on EVERYTHING) only asserts the offset resolves to a real substring. Span: 'skeptics say the bankruptcy fear is overblown.' LLM emits node: 'bankruptcy risk.' The substring 'bankruptcy' resolves -> node passes as grounded. Real entailment (NLI) is gated-tier ONLY, so ~99% of mass gets a weak check wearing a 'grounded' label that oversells faithfulness. Negation, hedging ('reportedly', 'denied', '~uncertain'), conditionals ('if X then Y'), and attribution ('the short-seller alleges') all survive cheap grounding intact.

Grounded-but-cherry-picked stance survives every check (the task's explicit 'grounded != unbiased'). Every node can pass S0 span-grounding AND S8 NLI entailment AND S2/S3 entity/rank coverage AND S6 cross-source dissent, while the ELEVATED SET is systematically skewed: from a 70%-cautionary filing the model elevates the 30% bullish sentences, each individually true and grounded. S2/S3 check WHICH entities/ranks appear; S6 checks cross-SOURCE dissent. NOTHING checks within-document stance/sentiment dispersion vs. the stance of what got elevated. The selection-of-which-grounded-facts-to-elevate bias -- the exact one the task names -- has no detector. Undisclosed.

The non-LLM omission baseline C shares the LLM's selection blindness (correlated error in the omission detector itself). C = NER + cashtag/ticker gazetteer + GDELT + event-lexicon. Gazetteers encode what is ALREADY popular; GDELT is EN/major-outlet biased; NER is weak on novel KR small-caps. These share the LLM's popularity + recency + language + canonical-naming blind spots. So C\E surfaces omissions only among things C already knows -- a genuinely novel, never-before-seen KR small-cap or a slang-only cashtag is missed by BOTH C and E together, and recall-at-span reads ~1.0 (false reassurance) precisely when omission is worst. The design files this under 'weak gazetteer = calibration'; it is actually structural correlated error, the same defect it rejects same-family debate for.

Estimated omitted-mass % is biased LOW because captures are positively correlated (VERIFIED). The Good-Turing / capture-recapture 'omitted-mass' number assumes independent captures. The LLM and non-LLM extractors read the SAME cleaned span and share popularity/recency/language failure modes = positive ascertainment covariance, which provably underestimates the hidden population (correlated captures -> negative bias in capture-recapture). So the human-facing 'omitted-mass %' systematically UNDERSTATES omission and reads as reassurance exactly when both extractors are blind together. A continuous number that is biased toward zero is worse than no number -- it manufactures false coverage confidence.

Salience gating is circular w.r.t. the pump it most needs to catch. D2 (price/volume coordinated-cluster detection) -- the one expensive consensus check -- is gated to ALREADY-high-salience contested items. But a thin-name pump is not high-salience UNTIL it works. The sophisticated adversary the design admits (single paraphrasing copier of a cherry-picked-TRUE seed) can be generalized: distributed paraphrasing copiers, staggered to beat temporal synchrony, on aged/diverse accounts to beat co-action, seeded from a genuinely-true Tier-1 filing fragment. This clears D1 (multiple roots), D3 (no shared idiosyncratic error -- they copy a true source), and D2 (no synchrony, sub-salience until it moves price), so the entire D-stack degrades to 'raw M visible' for the one attack the consensus machinery exists to stop.

Effective-independent count is a directional estimator biased toward BELIEVING consensus, and that direction is buried. The design concedes effective-independent is a lower bound on dependence (Dong VLDB 2009 good-source copier blind spot), which means it is an UPPER bound on independence -- it systematically OVER-states independence and UNDER-counts collusion. The integrity layer's own estimation error therefore points toward affirming consensus, the precise bias it exists to fight. This is disclosed as a tradeoff but NOT surfaced to the human as a known directional bias on the 'K effective-independent' number itself.
overEngineering: The standing eval/calibration apparatus is the real cost-cascade violation, and it is hidden in ops rather than runtime. The spec requires: hand-labeled KR+EN gold sets with B-cubed/pairwise P/R, abstain-rate monitoring, recall-spike monitoring, suppression-rate audits, planted-disclosure audits, retrospective price-move regressions, synthetic cutoff-leakage injection harness, and ~6 thresholds (salience, RBO, recall-at-k, materiality, independence discounts, gate) tuned 'without an oracle.' That is a research-team eval load standing up permanently to serve 1-2 consumers. The per-item runtime cost was tiered down correctly, but the cost did not leave the system -- it migrated into a continuous ops/eval burden that dwarfs the value to two humans. This is the strongest 'over-built' finding.

Entity-swap counterfactual probe (S8) is over-built even for the gated tier. It requires 2x extraction PLUS a separate real-world-fame model to interpret whether the delta 'tracks the name's fame' -- introducing a fame prior to detect a fame prior. The design itself open-questions a cheaper structural proxy (embedding-distance under name masking). For 2 consumers, defer the full probe; ship the cheap masking-distance proxy only.

S10 full CIB/coordinated-cluster system (multiplex network + community detection + price/volume cross-validation) is correctly self-marked 'Phase-2, highest false-positive.' Take the spec at its word and DEFER it -- do not build the most expensive, highest-FP component for a 2-user system until the cheaper D1/D3 stack demonstrably under-performs in production. Building it Phase-1 is premature.

Twelve+ distinct flag types (K-eff-of-M, origin/amplification trail, coordination annotation, minority-report, per-node ungrounded/parametric/name-sensitive tags, omitted-mass %, residual-critic list, gate-suppressed low-confidence band) draining to 1-2 human consumers is presentation over-engineering. Not a backend cost, but a consumer-cost violation: see residualBias.
residualBias: The human attention bottleneck re-collapses the distribution at the eyeball -- the deepest undisclosed residual. With 12+ flag types plus a 'gate-suppressed low-confidence' pile draining to 1-2 humans, flag fatigue forces the human to satisfice on the salience-ranked top. Consensus/salience collapse returns AT THE CONSUMER, downstream of every structural guarantee. The 'surface suppressed items as low-confidence' mitigation is invisible to two tired users -- it satisfies a mechanical coverage test while failing the actual anti-bias goal. The whole 'don't collapse the distribution' thesis is defeated by the last six inches between the screen and the human, and this is nowhere disclosed.

Incumbency/track-record bias is baked into the reused credibility-weighted PPR + TrustRank and propagates everywhere they are consumed (S3 ranking, S5 corroboration weights). Track-record weighting structurally suppresses genuine new entrants and emerging-but-unproven sources -- a real grassroots signal from a new credible voice is down-weighted as 'low credibility' indistinguishably from a pump. Only half-covered by 'credibility is bitemporal.' The integrity layer inherits and amplifies the credibility layer's conservatism bias.

Default-low-independence (D2/D3) is an explicit consensus-SUPPRESSING bias in the opposite direction (disclosed) -- but its interaction with incumbency bias above is NOT disclosed: a genuinely new, genuinely independent grassroots consensus gets hit TWICE (low credibility AND defaulted-low-independence), making the system structurally most blind to exactly the early true minority signal the human most needs.

Grounding gives faithfulness to SOURCE, not truth (disclosed) -- but the residual that follows is not: when a cherry-picked-true seed is laundered through paraphrasing copiers from a real filing, EVERY layer (grounding, NLI, D1 roots, D3 shared-error) affirms it, and the human sees high effective-independent corroboration of a true-but-misleading frame. Faithful + corroborated + grounded can all be green on a deliberately curated subset of true facts.
concreteFixes: Add a cheap within-document STANCE-DISPERSION detector at EXTRACT (closes the biggest undisclosed hole, #3, at cheap tier, consistent with the funnel). Run the already-present sentiment/stance classifier over (a) the full span union and (b) the elevated/ranked claim set; flag when the stance distribution of the elevated set diverges from the source document's stance distribution (e.g., 70% cautionary source -> 80% bullish elevated). Surface as a 'selection-skew' flag. This is the missing detector for 'which grounded facts got elevated,' parallel to S2/S3's 'which entities.'

Stop calling the NLI checker 'independent' and add a lexical-overlap-robustness gate. The frozen DeBERTa NLI shares the lexical-overlap shortcut (McCoy/HANS, VERIFIED), so its pass on high-overlap copied assertions is near-worthless. Fix: (a) route high-lexical-overlap span/assertion pairs to a HANS-style hard subset or a hypothesis-only-bias-corrected NLI head, not the vanilla checker; (b) explicitly downgrade S8's claimed independence in the spec; (c) at cheap tier, add a negation/hedge/attribution lexicon check on the span so polarity-inverted substrings (#2) cannot pass S0 as 'grounded.'

Correct the omitted-mass number for correlated captures, or stop reporting it as a point estimate. Since captures are positively correlated (VERIFIED negative bias), the Good-Turing/capture-recapture estimate is biased low. Fix: report it as an explicit LOWER BOUND on omission ('at least X% omitted'), add a SECOND genuinely-uncorrelated recall channel for the KR stream (GDELT is EN-biased -- add a KR-native source like a Korean disclosure/news extractor) so the captures are less correlated, and label the number 'lower bound, biased low when both extractors share blind spots.'

Make the directional bias of the independence estimator a first-class human-facing annotation. Print 'K effective-independent (UPPER bound on independence; collusion via good-source copying is undercounted)' wherever K is shown, so the human never reads K as a neutral count. This converts a buried tradeoff into the disclosed residual the task demands.

Hard-cap human-facing flags and make collapse auditable at the CONSUMER, not just the gate. For 1-2 consumers: collapse the 12 flag types into 2-3 composite badges (one grounding badge, one corroboration badge showing K-of-M, one dispersion badge linking to the minority report), and instrument whether the human ever opens the gate-suppressed / minority sections. If they don't, the distribution IS collapsed regardless of backend guarantees -- track 'minority-section open rate' as the real anti-bias KPI.

Break the salience-gating circularity for pumps by making D2's CHEAP precursors always-on at low cost. Run cheap temporal-synchrony + volume-spike co-occurrence as a CHEAP-tier signal on thin/illiquid names regardless of current salience (illiquidity is knowable a priori, before the pump works), so a forming pump can RAISE its own salience into the gate instead of needing to be salient first.

Trim to ship: defer S10 full CIB and the 2x entity-swap probe to Phase-2 (per the spec's own marking); ship the cheap name-masking embedding-distance proxy for name-sensitivity now; and scope the eval apparatus to ONE gold KR+EN set + abstain-rate + suppression-rate, deferring planted-disclosure audits and price regressions until the cheap stack proves insufficient. This keeps the cost cascade honest for 2 consumers without gutting the sound spine.
